summary: Rewrite install's scaffold to hoist every present frontmatter field into typed properties and move prose module-side, replacing the file()-verbatim own-path lift (0016)

edit.description: Rewrite `member_module_source` (and its caller `scaffold`) so a scaffolded member module hoists every field present in the discovered source's frontmatter (`frontmatter::Member::fields`, not just the description-trigger field `description_trigger_value` special-cases today) into its own typed TS property via a new generic JSON-value-to-TS-literal renderer, and moves prose module-side per 0016: a short body inline as a string literal, a document body written to a new scaffold-authored module-adjacent file (never re-pointing `file()` at the original `.claude/` path) referenced via `file(import.meta.url, "./<name>.md")`. Update this module's own doc comments (top-of-file, `scaffold`, `member_module_source`) which currently describe the retired own-path/file()-verbatim behavior.

Rewrite `representing_lifts_every_discovered_member_byte_stable_with_no_guard_claim` (its "file()'s own path — untouched" / "no member here is emit-owned" assertions invert: the first emit now regenerates each governed artifact canonically and IS guard/note/modeline-claimed), `re_representing_never_re_scaffolds_and_converges` (still converges, now via canonical re-emission not own-path no-op), and `a_deepened_member_with_its_own_asset_is_emit_owned_and_a_lifted_one_is_not` (the own-path/deepened distinction it exercises collapses — every scaffolded member is emit-owned from its first emit) to assert whole-conversion semantics; add coverage for a hoisted non-description field and for the inline-vs-module-adjacent-file prose split.

tests.asserts: a freshly scaffolded member module types every present frontmatter field as a TS property, its prose lands module-side (inline or a module-adjacent file, never the original `.claude/` path), and the first emit's projection is guard/note/modeline-claimed rather than an own-path passthrough

acceptance: A fresh `install --yes` scaffold's member modules type every present field and carry prose module-side; the first emit's projections are guard-claimed, never own-path passthrough; cargo test green.
notes: scoped at e920fa8, unblocked 2f87229 (SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP shipped); 0016 (specs/decisions/0016-install-installs-the-tool.md), specs/model/pipeline.md 'Install'. RETIRE-OWN-PATH-MACHINERY (blockedBy this) deletes the now-dead own_path detection/lock-column/guard-exemption once no live scaffold path emits own_path=true. `.temper/`'s own reconversion is outside build's writable fence (chain.ts) - human/ratification territory, not filed here.

summary: Rewrite acceptance.rs's two custom-kind tests off the dead KIND.md/PACKAGE.md format onto lock-declared kinds; today they pass vacuously via an always-on note, not the custom kind's own clause.

edit.description: `check_dispatches_the_spec_custom_kind_through_its_extractor_and_contract` and `check_reads_a_custom_kind_rooted_outside_specs` (plus the `author_custom_kind` helper and `SPEC_KIND_MD`/`SPEC_PACKAGE_MD` consts, lines ~204-342) author a custom kind by writing `.temper/kinds/<name>/KIND.md` + `.temper/packages/<name>/PACKAGE.md` — a file format nothing in src/ parses (grep confirms no `"kinds"`/`"packages"` path literal anywhere in src/; `kind.rs`'s own doc comment says custom kinds are `CustomKind::new` SDK constructs now, 'no `KIND.md` file format to parse it from'). Rewrite both tests to author their custom kind + advisory `max_lines` clause via a hand-written `.temper/lock.toml` `declaration.kind` + `declaration.clause` row pair instead (the shape `tests/session_start.rs`'s `a_custom_kind_synthesized_from_the_lock_resolves_its_requirement_with_no_false_admissibility_finding` already uses), and assert on the emitted diagnostic naming the clause (e.g. contains `max_lines` and the offending member's path) rather than the `--deny-advisories` exit-code flip alone — the flip fires on ANY corpus today (verified: a corpus with no custom kind at all still flips non-zero under `--deny-advisories`, since `coverage.checked` and `install.gate-installed` are always-on warn notes), so it proves nothing about the custom kind's own clause.

tests.asserts: a lock-declared custom kind's own advisory clause names itself in the diagnostic output for a violating member and is silent for a clean one, independent of the always-on coverage/gate-installed notes

acceptance: tests/acceptance.rs's custom-kind coverage exercises the live lock-declared kind mechanism with no `.temper/kinds`/`.temper/packages` file fixtures remaining anywhere in the suite; cargo test green.
notes: Residue sweep (job 4): grep confirms no 'kinds'/'packages' path literal anywhere in src/ - the KIND.md/PACKAGE.md custom-kind authoring format is fully dead (kind.rs: CustomKind is SDK-constructed, no file format). Verified empirically: a corpus with NO custom-kind fixture at all still flips non-zero under --deny-advisories (coverage.checked + install.gate-installed are always-on warn notes) - proving the two tests' current pass is unrelated to any custom-kind clause firing. scoped at 5264c0f.

summary: Rename the retired `floor` vocabulary (0001) to `default contract` across builtin-contract resolution, SDK exports, and the `why` verb's pinned narration

edit.description: Rename `builtin_floor`/`builtin_floors`/`BUILTIN_FLOOR_KINDS` (~lines 53-71) and their call sites (~280-300, 606, 634) to `default contract` naming (e.g. `builtin_default_contract`/`builtin_default_contracts`/`BUILTIN_DEFAULT_CONTRACT_KINDS`); reword the surrounding doc comments that say `floor`.

Rename `floor_from_rows` and the `floor: Contract` parameter of `effective` to `default contract` naming; rename the private test helper `floor()` and the test fns `effective_with_no_clause_rows_yields_the_floor_unchanged`, `effective_overrides_a_floor_clauses_severity_by_matching_identity`, `effective_ignores_a_row_with_no_matching_floor_clause`, `floor_from_rows_builds_a_custom_kinds_whole_floor`, `floor_from_rows_skips_a_row_it_cannot_lift`.
Reword the `why` verb's user-facing line (~424) `"Governing floor: its `{}` kind binds the `{}` floor, whose clauses check it.\n"` and its doc comment to say `default contract` instead of `floor`.

Rename the six exported clause arrays `skillFloor`/`commandFloor`/`agentFloor`/`ruleFloor`/`memoryAnthropicFloor`/`memoryAgentsMdFloor` and their `"The floor for `X`"` doc-comment headers to `default contract` naming.
Update the re-exported names and the adoption-example doc comment (`skillFloor`, ~line 8) to the renamed exports.
Update every import, assertion, and test description string referencing the six renamed exports.

Rename `mod floor_binding` and its test fns `a_memory_member_names_its_own_floor_never_the_skill_floor`, `a_builtin_name_resolves_to_its_bound_floor`, `a_floorless_kind_falls_back_to_its_own_name`; update the assertion substrings and the `READ-FLOOR-BINDING-DEFAULT`-tagged doc comments (~lines 3-4, 314-375) to match the new output wording.
Update the embedded `MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM` TS fixture's imports/keys (`agentFloor` etc., ~lines 65-86) and the surrounding doc comments to the renamed SDK exports.

Update the header comment's named SDK exports (`agentFloor` etc., ~lines 13-20) to match the rename; the `[[declaration.*]]` rows below are unaffected data, re-derived/re-embedded per the file's own regen instructions.

tests.asserts: `why`'s governing-contract narration says `default contract`, never `floor`, for a builtin-bound, custom-kind, and floorless-fallback member

acceptance: No live `floor`/`Floor` identifier, user-facing string, or test name for this concept remains in src/, tests/, or sdk/; the 5 pinned `read_verbs__why_*` snapshots regenerate to the new wording; cargo test and sdk test green.
notes: scoped at 77b2eb9 (residue sweep, job 4); 0001 retired `floor` for `default contract` (specs/builtins.md 'Default contracts' uses only the latter; corpus never says `floor`). Comment-only `floor` mentions elsewhere (builtin.rs, kind.rs, roster.rs, extract.rs, contract.rs, schema.rs, engine.rs's unrelated 'defensive floor', bundle.rs) are out of scope — no live identifier there, so they ride whichever entry next opens those files for a real reason.

summary: Rename the stale `posture` naming for the guard's block/warn/note behavior to `enforcement mode`, matching the settled term used elsewhere in the same files

edit.description: Reword `GUARD_COMMAND`'s doc comment (~103-109: "declared posture", "the posture is read from", "posture-independent") and the `guard` fn's doc comment (~603: "enforcement posture", one stale word amid otherwise-consistent `mode`/`EnforcementMode` wording) to say `enforcement mode`, matching this file's own already-consistent wording at ~116 and ~584.
Reword `AssemblyFactRow.value`'s doc comment (~1321: "the root member's declared enforcement posture") to "declared enforcement mode".

Rename `guard_reads_the_block_posture_from_the_lock_not_the_retired_manifest` and reword its doc comment plus the assertion message "the lock's `block` posture must block"; reword the neighboring `guard_defaults_to_warn_when_the_lock_is_absent`'s "default posture" doc comment; rename the `tmpdir("lock-posture-block")`/`tmpdir("lock-posture-absent")` labels — all to `mode` wording.

tests.asserts: the guard's block/warn tests and their names/labels read `mode`, never `posture`, consistent with `enforcement mode` used elsewhere in this file

acceptance: No `posture` referring to the guard's block/warn/note concept remains in src/install.rs, src/drift.rs, or tests/install.rs; cargo test green.
notes: scoped at 77b2eb9, resequenced 4b0122e (job 5). representation.md 'The root member' names this field `enforcement mode`. blockedBy INSTALL-WHOLE-CONVERSION (shared src/install.rs + tests/install.rs). RETIRE-OWN-PATH-MACHINERY is now blockedBy this entry instead, sharing src/drift.rs + tests/install.rs with it. Excludes drift.rs:192/main.rs:126's 'CI posture', read.rs's 'mixed-posture corpus', main.rs:144's lone comment (no live symbol).

summary: Delete the own-path detection, lock column, and guard exemption from drift/import/main now that whole conversion makes them structurally unreachable (0016)

edit.description: Remove `Projection.own_path`, `resolves_to_own_path()`, and the own-path branch in `emit_one` (`desired`/`second_pass` collapse to always `project_bytes(&projection.fields, &projection.body, &placements)`, never the verbatim-body fallback); drop `own_path` from the `RollupEntry` construction in `emit_program`; `emit_owned_targets` stops reading the lock's `own_path` key — every row it iterates is a target.
Remove `RollupEntry.own_path` and its write in `rollup_tables` (the `table["own_path"]` line) — the rollup's four columns (`name`, `source_path`, `source_hash`, `emit_hash`) are the whole row.
Remove the stale `own_path`-referencing doc comment at the guard call site (~line 329) describing the retired exemption.
Remove `guard_allows_a_file_carried_members_own_path_source_under_block` — it constructs a synthetic `own_path = true` lock row, a shape the lock no longer writes.

Remove `an_own_path_members_crlf_body_also_normalizes_to_lf`, which builds a synthetic own-path payload to exercise the now-deleted verbatim-body branch; if CRLF normalization on the ordinary field-rendered path lacks its own coverage elsewhere in this file, add an equivalent assertion over `project_bytes` instead of dropping the coverage outright.

schemaDelta: lock.toml's per-kind rollup rows drop the `own_path` column

tests.asserts: CRLF bodies still normalize to LF through the ordinary field-rendered projection path, with no synthetic own-path fixture

acceptance: `own_path` is absent from src/drift.rs, src/import.rs, and the emitted lock schema; cargo test green with no synthetic own-path fixtures remaining.
notes: scoped at e920fa8, resequenced 4b0122e (job 5): blockedBy changed from INSTALL-WHOLE-CONVERSION to RETIRE-POSTURE-VOCABULARY-FOR-ENFORCEMENT-MODE — both share src/drift.rs + tests/install.rs, so posture's rename must land first. 0016's explicit consequence ('own_path retires from drift, guard, and lock'). On pickup, grep for own_path first - if INSTALL-WHOLE-CONVERSION left a live path still writing own_path=true, hold rather than delete logic a real caller needs.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, now at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
